stories.headline: Trump says ceasefire is 'over' after US and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: Speaking ahead of a Nato summit, the president says US negotiators can keep talking with the Iranians "if they want".

stories.headline: Trump says ceasefire ‘over’ after US, Iran trade attacks
stories.summary: Trump made the announcement at a NATO summit after Iran attacked bases hosting US forces in Kuwait and Bahrain.

stories.headline: Oil spikes and European stock markets slide as Trump says Iran ceasefire over
stories.summary: Crude oil prices rose more than 6% to a two-week high after US President Donald Trump declared the interim accord to end the war with Iran "over", while fresh US military strikes renewed concerns about oil supplies through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Top Democrats press Maine Senate candidate to drop out of race over sexual assault allegation
stories.summary: Graham Platner denied the allegation, calling it "categorically false" but said he was "taking the time to reflect on the best path forward".

stories.headline: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is ‘over’
stories.summary: Oil price extends gains after US president says that negotiating with Tehran is a ‘waste of time’

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after attacks on tankers

stories.headline: Trump Says US-Iran Ceasefire Is ‘Over’ After Strikes | The Pulse 7/8/2026
stories.summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Mike Bell, RBC BlueBay Market Strategy Head; Ionela Ciolan, Wilfried Martens Centre for European Studies Research Officer  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: US Stock Futures Slide After Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Is Over
stories.summary: US equity futures fell as investors turned risk averse after President Donald Trump said the tentative ceasefire with Iran has ended, fueling concerns of a renewal in fighting.

stories.headline: U.S. Strikes Iran and Reimposes Sanctions After Tanker Attacks
stories.summary: The military operation came hours after the U.S. Treasury revoked a waiver allowing global sales of Iranian oil.

stories.headline: Trump makes fresh Greenland demand and threatens trade with Spain at Nato meeting – Europe live
stories.summary: US president says he is not happy with Nato and tells officials to ‘cut all trade with Spain’ Trump renews call for US to take over Greenland as he arrives for Nato summitAnd that ends their short briefing, with Trump saying he will be back later, and Rutte desperately throwing in that he will also do a press conference if anyone is interested in it please.Trump also heaps praise on Turkey’s Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, praising the Turkish military mig

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after tankers hit in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

stories.headline: These Maine Democrats Might Replace Graham Platner in the Senate Race
stories.summary: Mr. Platner has not yet withdrawn from the race after a rape allegation, but Democrats in the state and nationally are already exploring potential alternatives.

stories.headline: U.S. and Iran launch fresh strikes following attacks on commercial ships
stories.summary: Iran said it had targeted more than 80 U.S. military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, after the U.S. launched strikes in response to attacks on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Denmark says it will defend Greenland as Trump threatens to revive NATO crisis
stories.summary: Shortly after arriving at a NATO meeting in Turkey, Trump restarted the divisive fight, telling reporters that Greenland “should be controlled by the United States, not by Denmark.”

stories.headline: Trump says MoU with ‘vicious, violent’ Iran is over
stories.summary: Trump says MoU with 'vicious, violent' Iran is over

stories.headline: NATO summit live: Trump rips NATO allies; calls Spain ‘a terrible partner’
stories.summary: US president singles out Madrid for particular criticism calling it a 'terrible partner in NATO'.

stories.headline: NATO leaders to meet after Trump restates Greenland claim
stories.summary: Trump dashed hopes of a two-day summit by telling reporters the US should "control" Greenland. The Danish prime minister hit back, calling on the US to "respect Danish sovereignty".

stories.headline: Indian Stocks, Rupee Slide After Trump Reignites Iran Tensions
stories.summary: Indian stocks tumbled and the rupee weakened after oil prices gained following US President Donald Trump’s remark that the tentative ceasefire with Iran had ended after recent attacks on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Why have half a million Russians gone bankrupt amid Ukraine war?
stories.summary: European intelligence report warns of strain on Russia's banks, but experts say a full-blown crisis is unlikely

stories.headline: Funeral events for Iran’s slain Supreme Leader Khamenei under way in Iraq
stories.summary: Khamenei's coffin made its way through the streets of Najaf to the shrine of Imam Ali, Prophet Muhammad's son-in-law.

stories.headline: Trump attacks Spain calling it a 'wasted cause' and orders trade to stop
stories.summary: The US president had already threatened in March to take economic reprisals against Spain after Moncloa blocked the use of the Rota and Morón bases for Washington’s bombing campaign against Iran.

stories.headline: At least seven killed in fresh Russian strikes on Ukraine as NATO leaders meet in Turkey
stories.summary: Russia has hit the Ukrainian capital with several large deadly attacks over the last week, killing more than 50 this month in a spate of ballistic missile and drone strikes.

stories.headline: Will Le Pen rise again? French nationalist leader defiant after court's ruling
stories.summary: Within hours of a court of appeal confirming a guilty verdict, Marine Le Pen had already launched her presidential campaign.

stories.headline: Cubans protest after third nationwide power cut this year
stories.summary: While Cuban officials said that power had been mostly restored, those still left in the dark vented their anger.

stories.headline: A mountain of rubbish in Indonesia has been on fire for more than a week
stories.summary: Environmental activists say the blaze is a symptom of the country's growing waste crisis.

stories.headline: ‘Where do I get that from?’: UK asylum seekers face hefty repayment bill
stories.summary: Refugees and NGOs decry the government's plans to make people pay 10,000 pounds ($13,360) towards subsistence fees.

stories.summary: The US president has again threatened to sever trade ties with his Iberian partners, whose exports to the United States make up under 5% of their trade balance. Can Trump go further than tariffs?

stories.headline: Apple to buy $30bn of US-made chips from Broadcom
stories.summary: iPhone maker continues its attempt to curry favour with Donald Trump via investments in Colorado plant

stories.headline: Japan’s borrowing costs soar to 30-year high on debt fears
stories.summary: Worries over long-term spending plans help push bond yields to highest since 1996

stories.headline: Argentina to Repay $4 Billion, Defying Critics Who Doubted Tack
stories.summary: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible while the country refuses to tap global debt markets.

stories.headline: State Bank of India Draws Over $1.5 Billion in Offshore Deposits
stories.summary: State Bank of India has so far raised more than $1.5 billion from foreign-currency deposits under a special program launched last month for overseas citizens, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.headline: Bangladesh Explores Debut Sovereign Bond Sale Overseas
stories.summary: Bangladesh is studying a plan to issue its first-ever sovereign bond overseas, as the nation looks for ways to finance its record spending.

stories.headline: India Clears New Derivatives on Index Designed for Foreigners
stories.summary: India’s market regulator has approved the launch of derivatives on an index designed specifically for foreign investors.

stories.headline: Bernie Sanders Calls on Graham Platner to Drop Senate Bid After Rape Allegation
stories.summary: Mr. Sanders was one of the last remaining prominent allies of Graham Platner, the Maine Senate Democratic nominee, to call for him to drop out after a rape allegation.

stories.headline: 6 potential replacements for Graham Platner if he drops out of Senate race

stories.headline: Top Democrats pull Graham Platner endorsements, call on him to end Maine Senate campaign
stories.summary: The Democrat, who has withstood other scandals since launching his challenge to Sen. Susan Collins (R), is accused of sexual assault by a woman he previously dated.

stories.headline: Why European backlash over Trump intervention won't worry Infantino

stories.headline: Bomb explosions injure 18 in Damascus during Macron's visit

stories.summary: Stocks fell, bond yields spiked and oil rallied after President Donald Trump thrust geopolitical risks back into focus by declaring the ceasefire between the US and Iran to be over.

SpaceX stock dropped precipitously after its initial public offering, and Tesla faces a wave of lawsuits. stories.summary: The Trump administration sees the aid as more transactional - but some say it is not a fair trade.

stories.headline: Temasek-Backed Foundation Healthcare Falls in Singapore Debut
stories.summary: Foundation Healthcare Holdings Ltd. fell in its debut after a listing that was the largest of the year so far in Singapore outside real estate investment trusts.

stories.headline: Catnip lotion as effective as Deet at repelling mosquitoes, study finds
stories.summary: Researchers testing a cheap, homegrown oil in Uganda found what cats knew all along – it worked as well as the artificial chemical used globally A homegrown catnip lotion has proven “just as effective as Deet” as a mosquito repellant in trials carried out in Uganda.Catnip, or Nepeta cataria, is a common herb from the mint family. The chemical in the plant that causes feline euphoria – nepetalactone – also has insect-repelling properties but this

stories.headline: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 23 killed in Kyiv region
stories.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

stories.headline: Headteacher among four killed in deadliest Israeli attack on Lebanon since latest ceasefire
stories.summary: Three women and one man were travelling in a car after visiting their family home, Lebanese state media reported.

stories.headline: China tests missile in the Pacific hours after Australia-Fiji alliance signed

stories.headline: Typhoon Maysak kills two and forces thousands to evacuate in China
stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming daysA tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China.Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. stories.headline: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: Rodríguez defends Venezuela’s emergency earthquake response as number of bodies expected to soar

stories.headline: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas
